changes.value:
## What
Implement the main entry point that exposes all kanban operations as CLI subcommands and handles `kanban <path>` to open the GUI.

**File:** `kanban-cli/src/main.rs`

**CLI subcommands:** Build clap subcommands from kanban Operation trait metadata. Each operation becomes a `{verb}-{noun}` subcommand (e.g., `add-task`, `list-columns`, `move-task`). Use the same Lazy static pattern from `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs:46-148`.

**Dispatch:** Replicate the `execute_operation` match from `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs:382-804`. Map clap args to JSON, use `parse_input` to build `KanbanOperation`, match on `(Verb, Noun)` to construct typed operation structs, call `processor.process(&op, &ctx).await`.

**Open command:** `kanban .` or `kanban <path>` resolves path to absolute, URL-encodes, opens `kanban://open/<path>` via `open::that()`. Use `allow_external_subcommands(true)` â€” unrecognized subcommands that look like paths trigger open.

**Context resolution:** Use `KanbanContext::find(&cwd)` for operations, resolve explicit path for open command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `kanban init-board --name "Test"` creates a .kanban directory
- [ ] `kanban add-task --title "Hello"` creates a task
- [ ] `kanban list-tasks --column todo` lists tasks as JSON
- [ ] `kanban .` opens `kanban://open/...` URL (launches GUI app)
- [ ] `kanban /tmp/myproject` opens that path in GUI
- [ ] All kanban operations (board, column, swimlane, actor, task, tag, attachment, activity) work

## Tests
- [ ] Integration test: init board, add task, list tasks, complete task
- [ ] Unit test: path detection heuristic (`.`, `./foo`, `/absolute/path` â†’ open; `add-task` â†’ subcommand)
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-cli` passes
Implement kanban-cli operation dispatch and open command

+## What
+Implement the main entry point that exposes all kanban operations as CLI subcommands and handles `kanban <path>` to open the GUI.
+
+**File:** `kanban-cli/src/main.rs`
+
+**CLI subcommands:** Build clap subcommands from kanban Operation trait metadata. Each operation becomes a `{verb}-{noun}` subcommand (e.g., `add-task`, `list-columns`, `move-task`). Use the same Lazy static pattern from `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs:46-148`.
+
+**Dispatch:** Replicate the `execute_operation` match from `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs:382-804`. Map clap args to JSON, use `parse_input` to build `KanbanOperation`, match on `(Verb, Noun)` to construct typed operation structs, call `processor.process(&op, &ctx).await`.
+
+**Open command:** `kanban .` or `kanban <path>` resolves path to absolute, URL-encodes, opens `kanban://open/<path>` via `open::that()`. Use `allow_external_subcommands(true)` â€” unrecognized subcommands that look like paths trigger open.
+
+**Context resolution:** Use `KanbanContext::find(&cwd)` for operations, resolve explicit path for open command.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `kanban init-board --name "Test"` creates a .kanban directory
+- [ ] `kanban add-task --title "Hello"` creates a task
+- [ ] `kanban list-tasks --column todo` lists tasks as JSON
+- [ ] `kanban .` opens `kanban://open/...` URL (launches GUI app)
+- [ ] `kanban /tmp/myproject` opens that path in GUI
+- [ ] All kanban operations (board, column, swimlane, actor, task, tag, attachment, activity) work
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test: init board, add task, list tasks, complete task
+- [ ] Unit test: path detection heuristic (`.`, `./foo`, `/absolute/path` â†’ open; `add-task` â†’ subcommand)
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-cli` passes
\ No newline at end of file

-## What
Implement the main entry point that exposes all kanban operations as CLI subcommands and handles `kanban <path>` to open the GUI.

**File:** `kanban-cli/src/main.rs`

**CLI subcommands:** Build clap subcommands from kanban Operation trait metadata. Each operation becomes a `{verb}-{noun}` subcommand (e.g., `add-task`, `list-columns`, `move-task`). Use the same Lazy static pattern from `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs:46-148`.

**Dispatch:** Call the shared `swissarmyhammer_kanban::dispatch_operation()` (extracted in prior card). Map clap args â†’ JSON â†’ `parse_input` â†’ `Operation` â†’ `dispatch_operation()`. No duplication of the verb/noun match.

**Open command:** `kanban .` or `kanban <path>` resolves path to absolute, URL-encodes, opens `kanban://open/<path>` via `open::that()`. Use `allow_external_subcommands(true)` â€” unrecognized subcommands that look like paths trigger open.

**Context resolution:** Use `KanbanContext::find(&cwd)` for operations, resolve explicit path for open command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `kanban init-board --name "Test"` creates a .kanban directory
- [ ] `kanban add-task --title "Hello"` creates a task
- [ ] `kanban list-tasks --column todo` lists tasks as JSON
- [ ] `kanban .` opens `kanban://open/...` URL (launches GUI app)
- [ ] `kanban /tmp/myproject` opens that path in GUI
- [ ] All kanban operations (board, column, swimlane, actor, task, tag, attachment, activity) work

## Tests
- [ ] Integration test: init board, add task, list tasks, complete task
- [ ] Unit test: path detection heuristic (`.`, `./foo`, `/absolute/path` â†’ open; `add-task` â†’ subcommand)
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-cli` passes
\ No newline at end of file
+## What
Implement the kanban-cli entry point that is literally `sah tool kanban` as a standalone binary, plus an `open` command and banner.

**File:** `kanban-cli/src/main.rs`

**CLI structure:** Use the shared CLI generation utilities (extracted in prior card) to build the exact same noun-verb subcommand tree that `sah tool kanban` has:
- `kanban board init --name "Test"`
- `kanban task add --title "Hello"`
- `kanban task move --id "..." --column doing`
- `kanban column list`
- etc.

**Execution:** Use the shared argument extraction to build JSON args from clap matches, then call the kanban MCP tool's `execute()` directly (same as `sah tool kanban` does). No new dispatch code.

**Open command:** `kanban .` or `kanban <path>` resolves path to absolute, URL-encodes, opens `kanban://open/<path>` via `open::that()`. Use `allow_external_subcommands(true)` â€” unrecognized subcommands that look like paths trigger open.

**Banner:** Show on bare `kanban` or `kanban --help`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `kanban board init --name "Test"` behaves identically to `sah tool kanban board init --name "Test"`
- [ ] `kanban task add --title "Hello"` works
- [ ] `kanban .` opens `kanban://open/...` URL
- [ ] All noun-verb subcommands match `sah tool kanban` exactly

## Tests
- [ ] Integration test: init board, add task, list tasks via CLI
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-cli` passes
\ No newline at end of file
